results.title: Contract Award Data in SAM.gov | SAM.gov
excerpts:
# Contract Awards in SAM.gov

**Legacy Website**

GSA continues to create a more modern and unified user experience for the entities that want to do business or currently are doing business with the federal award community while reducing costs for the government and the taxpayer.

The Federal Procurement Data System’s ( [FPDS.gov](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/http:%2F%2Ffpds.gov%2F%3Futm_medium=email%26utm_source=govDelivery/1/01010198336a59b3-f4a84542-2057-49c3-8ce0-a421d67890ec-000000/44iFbGjWO_vok17i2MErJ3rL3LLaYMitXOH0KivG--c=414) ) capabilities will transition to SAM.gov on an incremental basis to make way for a modernized procurement data system within SAM.gov

**ezSearch**

##### **TO WATCH A TRAINING VIDEO ABOUT CONTRACT AWARD SEARCHING IN SAM.GOV, CLICK** [**HERE**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EfaaXKbyelM)

GSA has completed the transition of ezSearch functionalities to [SAM.gov](http://sam.gov) , and decommissioned ezSearch on February 24, 2026 .
The [contract award search](https://sam.gov/search) function in SAM.gov allows users to search federal procurement data and to filter by keyword, agency, and legal business name.  Results are available to anyone with a [SAM.gov](http://sam.gov) account, including contractors, agencies, and the public.

The federal government procurement data found in SAM.gov’s search is mostly used for policy and trend analysis.  Search results contain publicly available information about all unclassified contract actions exceeding the micro-purchase threshold and any modifications to those actions that change previously reported data, regardless of dollar value.

With the transition of ezSearch into SAM.gov, we also made some improvements to the functionality, including:

* Creating effective navigation between Contract Opportunities, Contract Awards and Subaward Reporting domains.
* Offering the search filters that are widely used.
* Developing Pickers for search filters, the functionality that does not exist in FPDS.gov.
* Developing search filters to be more intuitive and to allow partial searches which do not exist in [FPDS.gov](https://links-2.govdelivery.com/CL0/http:%2F%2Ffpds.gov%2F%3Futm_medium=email%26utm_source=govDelivery/1/01010198336a59b3-f4a84542-2057-49c3-8ce0-a421d67890ec-000000/44iFbGjWO_vok17i2MErJ3rL3LLaYMitXOH0KivG--c=414) .

NASA Specific Awards have not been transitioned to [SAM.gov](http://sam.gov) .  NASA Award data may be obtained from the [SAM Data Bank](https://sam.gov/reports/awards/standard) or FPDS Advanced Search.

**Atom Feed**

ATOM Feed will be replaced with the [SAM.gov Contract Awards API](https://open.gsa.gov/api/contract-awards/) in [open.gsa.gov](http://open.gsa.gov/) .

The new API will include the following enhancements:

* Direct & On-Demand Queries
* Advanced, Granular Search
* Flexible Formats (JSON, CSV)
* Richer Data (Deleted Contracts, etc.)

results.title: Contract Data | SAM.gov
excerpts:
**Government users:** We encourage you to request a contract data role if you need access to restricted data such as DoD’s 90-day award data.

* [Request a federal role](https://sam.gov/profile/request-role)

## Contract Award Reports

There are pre-formatted standard reports, advanced Ad Hoc reports, and controlled Administrative reports within the Data Bank.

* [List of available standard reports](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0029880)
* [What are contract data ad hoc reports?](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp/en/what-are-contract-data-ad-hoc-reports?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=dc983a0c975d66905f6ef21de053af8d)
* [What are the available contract data static reports?](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp/en/what-are-the-available-contract-data-static-reports?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=06e15a60478dea50da6ff9ee626d43df)
* [Reference guide for standard reports](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0029881)
* Go to Contract Award Reports

## Manage Contract Awards

Federal users can manage contract award records by selecting the "manage contract awards" button and logging in with the legacy FPDS credentials.

* [Frequently Asked Questions](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_category&kb_category=03d35e9a1b2a2a900033c915624bcbfa)
* [Go to Contract Award Login](https://secure.login.gov/openid_connect/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=urn:gov:gsa:openidconnect.profiles:sp:sso:gsa:prod_fpds&scope=openid+email&redirect_uri=https://www.fpds.gov/common/jsp/loginsso.jsp&nonce=XIdVv6LZi1tVvD6AiILd99ZbIE8ozViN_iJcc_PFpW8&state=fZ23fA9zsv5gPwTyd-dmMiTa3gnsaE2q1UrQ0KD9jLA&acr_values=http://idmanagement.gov/ns/assurance/loa/1)

## Connect to Data Services

* [How to use data services](http://fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp/en/how-do-i-use-data-services?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0029694)
* [Go to public contract award API](https://www.fpds.gov/fpdsng_cms/index.php/en/worksite.html)
## System Accounts

* [Creating a system account](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0035680)
* [Renewing a system account Password](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB0017666)
* [Go to public contract award API](https://www.fpds.gov/fpdsng_cms/index.php/en/worksite.html)

## Help

* [Frequently Asked Questions](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_category&kb_category=bb9f7a401b4f64108aa3a8eae54bcb1c&kb_id=f66d8e6cdb76d4100d73f81d0f9619c6)
* [Glossary Terms](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_category&kb_category=d9cfb6801b4f64108aa3a8eae54bcbc5&kb_id=f66d8e6cdb76d4100d73f81d0f9619c6)
* [Videos](https://www.fsd.gov/gsafsd_sp?id=kb_category&kb_category=63afba401b4f64108aa3a8eae54bcb13&kb_id=f66d8e6cdb76d4100d73f81d0f9619c6)

[SBIR.gov](https://sbir.gov) has transitioned to a new and more secure identity verification and login feature. You may experience temporary delays when accessing your accounts.

# SBIR/STTR - America's Seed Fund - Powered by SBA

## America's Seed Fund
 ... 
The SBIR and STTR programs fund a portfolio of startups and small businesses across technology areas and markets to stimulate technological innovation, meet Federal research and development (R&D) needs, and increase commercialization to transition R&D into impact.

* Program Overview
* Participating Agencies

### Is SBIR right for me?

* I'm a small business with fewer than 500 employees
* My company's technology could help support government missions
* I'm looking for equity-free funding to develop my ideas

Find Out More
